name: Trimethylglycine
summary: Methyl donor that reliably lowers homocysteine biochemically, with uncertain clinical outcome benefit.
description: Betaine supports homocysteine remethylation to methionine, with downstream methylation capacity that may influence vascular and metabolic function.
safetyNotes: Human intervention data for supplemental betaine (trimethylglycine) remain limited, and a regulatory assessment did not establish safety at the applicant's proposed 2.5 g/day novel-food use. Long-term, pregnancy, lactation, disease-specific, and interaction evidence remains insufficient.
